steps.title: Track Daniel's repeated actions
steps.content: The passage repeatedly shows Daniel keeping the petition close, watching for unsigned tribal members, and approaching people wherever they are. These details build a portrait of his determined effort to collect signatures.

steps.title: Reject unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice A invents changing attitudes over time. Choice B claims enthusiasm from tribal members, but the passage focuses on Daniel's pursuit of them. Choice D invents refusals and dislike of Daniel. The remaining choice matches his commitment to the petition. The answer is C.
